data.title: Bass Pro Shops and White River Marine Group acquires Hobie Water Sports brand, moving production to Lebanon, Missouri. New job opportunities.
data.description: A renowned outdoor and conservation-centered company in North America is transferring production of Hobie products from Mexico to Lebanon, Missouri. The announcement was made on September 25, 2025, in Springfield, Missouri. Bass Pro Shops and White River Marine Group revealed that they have acquired Hobie, a well-known water sports brand. This move signifies a significant investment in the Lebanon facility and is expected to create new job opportunities in the region. The decision to bring Hobie production to Missouri underscores the commitment to local manufacturing and reinforces the company's dedication to quality and innovation.

data.title: Research report on potential impact of Trump 2.0 policies in global auto industry. Highlights 25% tariff on vehicle imports.
data.description: In Dublin, a report titled "Strategic Analysis of Trump 2.0 Policies and their Potential Impact on the Global Automotive Industry, 2025" has been recently included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. On March 26, 2025, President Donald Trump of the United States enforced a 25% tariff on the importation of passenger vehicles, light trucks, and specific automotive components such as engines, transmissions, powertrain parts, and electrical components. data.title: GeoPark becomes operator of 2 blocks in Vaca Muerta with 100% working interest, acquired from Pluspetrol in Argentina.
data.description: In Bogota, Colombia, GeoPark Limited (NYSE: GPRK), a prominent energy company in Latin America with 20+ years of experience, disclosed a new agreement with Pluspetrol S.A. to acquire a 100% operated WI in the Loma Jarillosa Este and Puesto Silva Oeste blocks. These blocks, situated in Neuquen Province, Argentina, are focused on extracting black oil from the Vaca Muerta formation. This strategic move marks GeoPark's continued expansion in the region.

data.title: Global advanced wound care market projected to reach $11.84 billion in 2024; CAGR of 5.3% expected in 2024-2030. AI-based devices and remote monitoring driving adoption.
data.description: A recent report has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings titled "Advanced Wound Care Industry Driving Transformational Growth by Developing Digital Wound Management Solutions, Global, 2024-2030." The report forecasts a revenue estimate of $11.84 billion for the base year 2024. In the advanced wound care management market, a CAGR of 5.3% is anticipated during the study period 2024-2030. The industry is experiencing significant advancements with the introduction of sensor-based wearables and handheld devices.
